description: Basic principles controlling isotope distribution, including natural abundances, radiogenic decay, equilibrium and kinetic isotope effects. Applications of the principles in the fields of: Earth history - global processes and chronology; mineralization - diagenesis, catagenesis; hydrogeology and characterization of water and air masses; and biogeochemistry and biological fractionation isotopes.

groupFilter1.name: School of Earth and Ocean Sciences

preAndCorequisites:
• Complete all of:
    • EOS240 - Geochemistry (1.5)
  • or permission of the department.
supplementalNotes: • Credit will be granted for only one of EOS 335, EOS 430.
title: Isotopes in Natural Sciences
